column6: Baseline: 1480 weak terms (89.2%), 1282 zero-evidence (77.3%), avg 0.73 evidence/term. 10 harvest runs over 39.4h = 0.25 runs/h. 79/1659 terms attempted (4.8%). 105/120 queries OK (87.5%), 5357 comments collected. Evidence files contain real Bilibili comments.

column3: Scraper is effective but throughput is critically slow — only 79/1659 terms attempted after 39.4 hours (0.25 runs/hour). 5 HTTP 412 rate limits + 7 timeouts (180s each) in 10 runs. At this rate, harvesting all 1480 weak terms would take ~25 weeks.
column6: Re-analysis of run timestamps reveals: Runs 1-3 were Jun 27, then a 25.6-HOUR GAP (no automation), then Runs 4-9 on Jun 28-29 at ~3 runs/hour. Active throughput is fine (~3 runs/h, 500+ evidence items/run). The illusion of slowness comes from the idle gap.

column3: No BILIBILI_COOKIE set — crawler runs as anonymous guest with synthetic cookies (buvid3/buvid4/b_nut/_uuid). This makes Bilibili's WAF more likely to return HTTP 412 on certain query patterns, especially multi-word search queries with "评论区 热评" suffixes.
column6: server/services/bilibiliCrawler.js:474-504 — ensureCookies() checks BILIBILI_COOKIE env var, falls back to random synthetic cookies when absent. All 5 HTTP 412 errors occurred on evidence/evasion family queries. Guest sessions trigger stricter rate limiting. However, impact is minor (5/120 queries = 4.2%).

column3: Exhausted terms are stuck because the exhausted fallback templates are too generic, not because terms are genuinely un-attestable. Successful queries use family-specific contexts ("争议" for evasion, "梗" for attack, "讨论" for cooperation), but exhausted fallbacks use bare "{term} 热评" with no context. Also 6/20 exhausted terms already have evidence (evidenceAtPlanTime > 0).
column6: Successful: "吃瓜 讨论 评论区 热评" (+6 evidence), "复制 评论区 梗 热评" (+2). Failed: "有链接吗 热评" (0), "也许 热评" (0), "呵呵 热评" (0). DEFAULT_EXHAUSTED_SUGGESTION_TEMPLATES (line 44-53) are generic "{term} 热评" / "{term} 回复" — no family context.

column3: The harvest IS effective — during active periods (Runs 4-9), it averages 394 evidence items/run at ~3 runs/hour. But there was a 25.6-hour idle gap between Run 3 (Jun 27 21:46) and Run 4 (Jun 28 23:22) when no automation was running. Evidence persistence is working: all 126 evidence files updated in last 24h. The scraper is genuinely effective when running.
column6: Run-by-run analysis: Run 5 = 802 comments, 446 evidence terms, 658 evidence items. Run 7 = 728 comments, 420 terms, 608 items. All 126 evidence files updated 2026-06-29T01:44-01:46. The "playing around" impression comes from the 25.6h gap + 20 terms stuck in retry loops with generic queries.
